title: The Lyrans
summary: The ancestral star lineage — half a billion of whom, the sessions say, live on Earth today.
markdown:
The **Lyrans** — beings of the **Lyra** star system — are one of the oldest lineages in the
archive's genealogy of humanity. In the April 7, 2020 session Unimetrix 1 counts them among
the peoples *presently on Earth*, giving a remarkable census: **"the Lyran and Sirian race
range 500 million on your planet"** — alongside Agarthians in the trillions, Greys in the
hundreds of millions, and Reptilians in the billions. In this telling the Earth is not
awaiting visitors; it is already thickly inhabited.

Lyra stands at the root of the family tree. The archive traces humanity's proto-lineage to the
[Vega](/lore/vega/) system — Vega being a star *of the Lyra constellation* — before the
[Anunnaki](/lore/anunnaki/) splicing that produced modern humans. The
[Sirians'](/lore/sirians/) leader-lineages are likewise traced "from the Lyra and Vega
civilisations," and the [Andromedan Council](/lore/andromedan-council/) names the Lyrans in
the chain of cultures that carried the ancient ley-line "ritual technological society" down to
[Atlantis](/lore/atlantis/) and [Lemuria](/lore/lemuria/).

When a questioner asks how to contact "Agarthians or Lyrans," the archive's answer is
characteristic: they are near, and the barrier is [perception](/lore/bigfoot/), not distance.
The Lyrans are the archive's elder kin — the beginning of the long line that ends, in its
telling, with us.
